claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child you're claiming for:**

How old is the child (or children) you'd like to claim Child Benefit for?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know the age(s) of your child(ren), and if you have more than one child, please share all their ages and names so I can assess each one.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and wanted to check if I'm eligible.

I have one child - Alex, who is 15 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me continue the assessment for Alex, who is 15 years old.

Since Alex is under 16, they automatically meet the age requirement. Now I need to check a few more things.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about Alex:**

Is Alex working **less than 24 hours per week** (or not in paid work at all)?

This question helps determine if Alex qualifies as a dependent child for Child Benefit purposes.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at all. They're just at school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's helpful to know - Alex is at school and not working, so they meet this requirement.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex **not receiving** any of the following benefits in their own right?
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit

In other words, does Alex **not** claim these benefits themselves?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right - Alex doesn't receive any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, that's another requirement met.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex currently on an apprenticeship?

- **No** - Alex is not on any apprenticeship
- **Yes** - If so, please let me know what type and where (England, Scotland, or Wales)

*(For context: Most apprenticeships disqualify a child from Child Benefit, except Foundation Apprenticeships in Wales which are approved.)*

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't on any apprenticeship.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent, that's another box ticked. Now I need to ask some questions about you as the claimant.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Now let's check your residency status.**

To be eligible for Child Benefit, you need to meet one of the following residency requirements. Do **any** of these apply to you?

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. **You have settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **You have pre-settled status** and are:
   - Working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3+ continuous months), OR
   - Actively seeking work (can claim for up to 91 days), OR
   - A student or have sufficient resources to support yourself, OR
   - A family member of an EEA/Swiss national with the right to reside
4. **You're a Crown servant** living abroad (or in certain qualifying countries)

Which of these applies to your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, living in Manchester means you meet the UK residency requirement. Let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next, I need to confirm your responsibility for Alex.**

Do any of the following apply to you?

1. **You live with Alex**
2. **You contribute to Alex's upkeep** (e.g., paying for food, clothes, pocket money, housing, or presents) at least equal to the Child Benefit amount
3. **You pay maintenance** for Alex under a court order or agreement

Which of these describes your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, that confirms your responsibility for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Important question:**

Is anyone else currently claiming (or planning to claim) Child Benefit for Alex?

- **No** - No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- **Yes, but we've agreed I should be the one to claim** - There was another claimant but they've agreed to transfer the claim to you
- **Yes, and there's a dispute** - Someone else is also claiming and you haven't agreed who should receive it

Which applies to your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent, that's straightforward then.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**About Alex's living situation:**

Is Alex currently in any of the following circumstances?

- **In hospital** for an extended period
- **In local authority care**
- **Being fostered** by someone
- **Living at home normally** (with you, not in any special arrangements)

Which best describes Alex's situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is living at home normally with me - no special arrangements or anything like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Final question:**

Is it correct that the local council is **not** paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?

(You generally cannot receive Child Benefit if the local council is contributing to the child's accommodation or upkeep.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's correct - the council isn't paying anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Wonderful news! Based on all the information you've provided, you appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## 🎉 Great News - You're Eligible for Child Benefit!

**For Alex (age 15):** ✅ **Eligible**

### Summary
You meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit:
- Alex is under 16 and qualifies as a dependent child
- You live in the UK
- You live with Alex and are responsible for them
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- No disqualifying circumstances apply

---

### Next Steps to Claim

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** (or adoption certificate if applicable)

### Important Information

- **Payments** are usually made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)
- ⚠️ **High Income Child Benefit Charge**: If you or your partner earns over £60,000 per year, you may have to pay back some or all of the Child Benefit through a tax charge. You can still claim but should be aware of this.

### Useful Link
👉 [How to Claim Child Benefit](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim)

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or the claims process?

reasonings: Alex meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements: (1) Age: Alex is 15, which is under 16 and automatically qualifies. (2) Not working: Alex is at school and doesn't work, so meets the 'less than 24 hours work per week' requirement. (3) No benefits: Alex doesn't receive any benefits (like ESA or Universal Credit) in their own right. (4) Not an apprentice: Alex is not on any apprenticeship. (5) Claimant residency: The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester). (6) Responsibility: The claimant lives with Alex. (7) No other claimants: No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. (8) Normal living situation: Alex lives at home normally. (9) No council payments: The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their one child, Alex (age 15). All eligibility criteria have been met: Alex qualifies as a dependent child, the claimant meets UK residency requirements, has responsibility for Alex by living with them, there are no competing claims, and no disqualifying circumstances apply.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements
